<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).

<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).

<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).

**Deities**

[Frenzied Revelry](/Spells.aspx?ID=1169)

You feast mightily and can shake off the effects of overindulging.
<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).

<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).

<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).

<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).

Apocryphal Domain Spells Source Dark Archive pg. 142 Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones. Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells.

<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).

<title level="3">Apocryphal Domain Spells</title>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 142

Not all clerical traditions are taught among the orthodoxy. Some are whispered in secret, passed down from a priest to trusted acolytes or scribed in tomes left behind by splinter factions. Some apocryphal spells are considered heretical and suppressed, but others have fallen out of use or are specific to a region or sect. These apocryphal spells usually rely on more abstract associations between divine power and a certain effect than those seen in orthodox ones.

[Click here for the full rules on Apocryphal Domain Spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=1728).
